---
title: "DICoP Annual Anomaly Detection"
subtitle: "EMS and Trauma Registry Reporting | 2020–2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: revealjs-letterbox/_extensions/EmilHvitfeldt/letterbox/letterbox.scss
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: false
    incremental: false
    transition: fade
    extensions:
      - letterbox
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  cache: false
---

```{julia setup}

#| label: setup
#| 
###_____________________________________________________________________________
# Setup for the anomaly detection project to leverage Tidier and DotEnv
# Setup for the environment variables to be used to secure filepaths
###_____________________________________________________________________________

using Pkg

# Ensure required packages are available
Pkg.activate(".");
Pkg.instantiate();

# only need to install packages the first time
# Pkg.add(["Tidier", "TidierPlots", "TidierDates", "Dates", "DotEnv", "CSV", "XLSX", "DataFrames", "Quarto", "PrettyTables", "Statistics", "Distributions", "Random", "Plots", "StatsBase", "StatsPlots"]);

# Load packages
using Tidier
using TidierDates
using Dates
using TidierPlots
using DotEnv
using CSV
using XLSX
using DataFrames
using Quarto
using PrettyTables
using Random
using Statistics
using Distributions
using Plots
using StatsBase
using StatsPlots

# get custom functions
include("./Julia/utils.jl");

# Load .env file into ENV[]
DotEnv.load!();

# Assign environment variables (no paths yet)
iowa_trauma_registry_counts_path = ENV["IOWA_TRAUMA_REGISTRY_COUNTS_PATH"];
iowa_ems_registry_counts_path = ENV["IOWA_EMS_REGISTRY_COUNTS_PATH"];

```

```{julia dist-setup}

simulate_and_plot("normal")
simulate_and_plot("poisson")
simulate_and_plot("quasipoisson")
simulate_and_plot("nb")

```

# Introduction

## Project Purpose
- Annual anomaly detection for EMS agencies and trauma centers.
- Use of distributional methods and practical thresholds.
- Overview of data, methodology, and results.

# Data Description

## Source and Structure
- Annual counts of record submission (2020–2026).
- Stable data generating process.

# Probability Distributions

## Refresher
- Normal
- Poisson
- Negative Binomial
- Why quasi-Poisson was not used.

# Anomaly Detection Methods

## Z‑Score
- Year-over-year differences.
- Approximate normality.

## Percent Change
- Operational thresholding.

## NB–Poisson Prediction Interval
- Distribution-based expected ranges.

# Results

## Summary of Findings
- Facilities flagged by each detector.
- Agreement between detectors.

# Future Extensions

## Alternative Time Scales
- Weekly or monthly modeling.
- Regression-based anomaly detection.

# Appendix

## Julia Code Examples

```julia
# snippets included here
```
